data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-12T09:04:44.321Z</current_datetime>

I have this MSTest data-driven test using object arrays for DynamicData. Is there a more type-safe way to do this in modern MSTest?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a more type-safe way to use `DynamicData` in modern MSTest, moving away from `IEnumerable<object[]>`. This is about MSTest's support for `ValueTuple` in `DynamicData`, which was introduced in MSTest v3+.

In modern MSTest (v3+), you can use `IEnumerable<(T1, T2, ...)>` (ValueTuples) instead of `IEnumerable<object[]>`, which gives you compile-time type safety.

Let me provide a concise answer.
